description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages install, test, build, review, and deploy, using node:alpine as the global image and python:latest for the AWS jobs, and cache the node_modules directory. Add an installit job in the install stage that runs "npm install". Add a testtime job in the test stage that runs "set CI=true&&npm test". Add a buildit job in the build stage that runs "npm run build", stores the build/ directory as an artifact named "${CI_JOB_NAME}_${CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME}" that expires in one week, uploads it on success, and excludes the branch "branchess". Add a start_review job in the review stage for all branches except master that installs awscli, copies the build/ folder to the S3 bucket $S3_BUCKET_NAME_FEATURE_BRANCH under $CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG, and creates a review environment with a URL and an on_stop link to stop_review; add a stop_review manual job that installs awscli, removes the same S3 folder, stops the review environment, sets GIT_STRATEGY to none, and runs only on branches except master. Finally, add deploytoaws_staging and deploytoaws_production jobs in the deploy stage that run only on the master branch, are manual, install awscli, copy the build/ folder to their respective S3 buckets ($S3_BUCKET_NAME_STAGING and $S3_BUCKET_NAME_PRODUCTION), and define production and staging environments with URLs based on the bucket names, using the AWS credentials and region variables defined at the pipeline level.
